user.title: MiWiFi Router
user.description:
Set up the Xiaomi MiWiFi Router integration.

ℹ️ Speed/traffic units can be changed later in Options, but changing them will trigger sensor entity re-creation and historical data will be lost.

data.force_hash_algo: Password hash algorithm (Auto-detect is recommended; manually specify only if login fails)
data.speed_unit: Realtime speed sensor unit (Auto = B/s, no conversion)
data.total_unit: Total traffic sensor unit (Auto = B, no conversion)

devices.title: Select devices to add sensors for
devices.description: Create independent speed and traffic sensors for selected devices. You can skip this and configure later.

error.invalid_auth: Wrong password or hash algorithm mismatch (try manually specifying SHA1/SHA256)

init.title: MiWiFi Router Options
init.description:
Adjust polling intervals and select devices to add sensors for. Unchecked devices will have their sensors automatically removed after reload.

⚠️ Changing speed/traffic unit triggers sensor entity re-creation; the historical data for those entities will be lost (a confirmation prompt will appear after submit).

data.force_hash_algo: Password hash algorithm (Auto-detect is recommended; manually specify only if login fails)
data.speed_unit: Realtime speed sensor unit (history data will be lost if changed)
data.total_unit: Total traffic sensor unit (history data will be lost if changed)

confirm_unit_change.title: Confirm Unit Change
confirm_unit_change.description:
⚠️ Unit change detected

{changes}

Changing the unit triggers sensor entity re-creation; the historical state data for those entities will be lost (HA limitation: state_class entities cannot dynamically change native_unit).

✅ Long-term statistics (statistics table) are not affected
✅ Energy Dashboard continues to work
✅ raw_b attribute always preserves the original byte value

If you accept the history data loss, check "Confirm" and submit.

data.confirm: I understand the history data will be lost, confirm to continue
